fields.narrative_intro: *An unhinged person and a bewildered person debate a Wikipedia article about software engineering inside a Wikipedia edit war.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
UNHINGED PERSON: "I'm updating 'software engineer' to include woodworking."
BEWILDERED PERSON: "...why?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
UNHINGED PERSON: "Claude Code one-shotted a race condition. Two days of work. Gone."
BEWILDERED PERSON: "Okay but that's not—"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
UNHINGED PERSON: "Ten years of payments expertise. Reduced to one surviving word: 'taste.'"
UNHINGED PERSON: "Dovetail joints require TASTE."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The bewildered person stares blankly at the screen. The edit history shows 47 rapid revisions to the article in the last 90 seconds. A Sentry MCP alert badge flashes ominously in the background.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
BEWILDERED PERSON: "Did DataDog MCP just auto-revert your edit?"
UNHINGED PERSON: "It one-shotted my escape plan."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The Wikipedia article now reads: 'Software Engineer (see also: Woodworking). Survivors: taste.' The edit was made by user Claude_Code_Bot.]

narrative_summary: A desperate engineer tries to update Wikipedia to legitimize his woodworking pivot, only for the very AI tools that eroded his career to auto-revert his escape plan.
rationale: Candidate 4 earns its ending — the AI tools that eroded the engineer's career literally auto-reverting his woodworking escape plan is a structurally tight escalation with a genuinely surprising payoff, and 'It one-shotted my escape plan' lands as a real punchline rather than a restatement. The specificity of Claude_Code_Bot as the Wikipedia editor and the Sentry MCP alert badge ties it tightly to the actual story without feeling like a listicle of product names.
story_title: LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
